item.title: Can We Create Artificial Gravity?
item.description: A look at how scientists and engineers explore ways to simulate gravity, including rotation and acceleration, for use in space travel.

media:content.media:title: Can We Create Artificial Gravity?
media:content.media:description: A look at how scientists and engineers explore ways to simulate gravity, including rotation and acceleration, for use in space travel.

item.title: How Longitude Defines Time Zones
item.description: A clear explanation of how lines of longitude are used to divide Earth into time zones, helping standardize time across different regions.

media:content.media:title: How Longitude Defines Time Zones
media:content.media:description: A clear explanation of how lines of longitude are used to divide Earth into time zones, helping standardize time across different regions.

item.title: How Many Ways Can You Shuffle a Deck?
item.description: A simple look at the mathematics behind card shuffling and why the number of possible arrangements is far larger than most people expect.

media:content.media:title: How Many Ways Can You Shuffle a Deck?
media:content.media:description: A simple look at the mathematics behind card shuffling and why the number of possible arrangements is far larger than most people expect.

item.title: Why Earth’s Temperature Is Rising
item.description: A clear look at the factors contributing to global warming, including greenhouse gases, human activity, and natural climate patterns.

media:content.media:title: Why Earth’s Temperature Is Rising
media:content.media:description: A clear look at the factors contributing to global warming, including greenhouse gases, human activity, and natural climate patterns.

item.title: What It Takes to Reach a Star Trek–Like Future
item.description: An exploration of the science and technology needed to approach the future imagined in Star Trek, from space travel to advanced energy systems.

media:content.media:title: What It Takes to Reach a Star Trek–Like Future
media:content.media:description: An exploration of the science and technology needed to approach the future imagined in Star Trek, from space travel to advanced energy systems.

item.title: Dr.Hubble Discovered The Andromeda Galaxy
item.description: A look at Edwin Hubble’s role in confirming that the Andromeda Galaxy lies beyond the Milky Way, helping expand our understanding of the universe.

media:content.media:title: Dr.Hubble Discovered The Andromeda Galaxy
media:content.media:description: A look at Edwin Hubble’s role in confirming that the Andromeda Galaxy lies beyond the Milky Way, helping expand our understanding of the universe.

item.title: What Causes Shadows in Space?
item.description: An explanation of how shadows form in space, including eclipses and how light interacts with planets, moons, and other objects.

media:content.media:title: What Causes Shadows in Space?
media:content.media:description: An explanation of how shadows form in space, including eclipses and how light interacts with planets, moons, and other objects.

item.title: Does Chuck Know the Quadratic Formula?
item.description: A light, educational moment exploring the quadratic formula and how it’s used to solve equations, with a simple and engaging approach to math.

media:content.media:title: Does Chuck Know the Quadratic Formula?
media:content.media:description: A light, educational moment exploring the quadratic formula and how it’s used to solve equations, with a simple and engaging approach to math.

item.title: Why Fusion Produces More Energy Than Fission
item.description: A clear explanation of how nuclear fusion and fission work, and why fusion releases more energy by combining atoms instead of splitting them.

media:content.media:title: Why Fusion Produces More Energy Than Fission
media:content.media:description: A clear explanation of how nuclear fusion and fission work, and why fusion releases more energy by combining atoms instead of splitting them.

item.title: Do We Need New Math for the Universe?
item.description: An exploration of whether current mathematics is enough to explain the universe or if new frameworks are needed to describe complex physical phenomena.

media:content.media:title: Do We Need New Math for the Universe?
media:content.media:description: An exploration of whether current mathematics is enough to explain the universe or if new frameworks are needed to describe complex physical phenomena.

item.title: Early Satellites and Their Purpose
item.description: A look at the first satellites and how early designs focused on communication, shaping the global networks we rely on today.

media:content.media:title: Early Satellites and Their Purpose
media:content.media:description: A look at the first satellites and how early designs focused on communication, shaping the global networks we rely on today.
